rationale: Both R5 round-1 falsifiability blockers are addressed in commit 18d7ea3f. The children-query AC now explicitly counts internal/phase's listJSONFn via phase.SetListJSONForTest, and it names stubChildrenByStatus as the complete-package stub to re-point. The byte-identical setup claim now has a full-equality per-agent test requirement and matching validation proof, not only strings.Contains-style coverage. I re-scanned the renumbered requirements and acceptance criteria; the stale .golangci.yml removal is cleanly out of scope, and the remaining ACs are concrete enough to fail before/pass after through named commands, tests, grep checks, or call-count assertions.

findings.detail: The revised spec requires exactly one bd list --parent subprocess to be asserted by counting calls on internal/phase's listJSONFn seam installed via phase.SetListJSONForTest, and explicitly says internal/complete's seam is no longer invoked for the children query. Repository verification: phase.SetListJSONForTest exists in internal/phase/derive.go:33, and stubChildrenByStatus is the old complete-package listJSONFn stub at internal/complete/complete_test.go:535 that the spec directs implementers to re-point.

The revised spec requires a per-agent full equality assertion for produced managed-doc content on non-symlinked targets, derived from the expected block constants, for claude, codex, and copilot. The validation proofs also name the managed-doc equality test. This makes the byte-identical claim falsifiable rather than relying on strings.Contains checks.

The renumbered requirements remain paired with concrete acceptance checks: deadcode -test scope and allowed exceptions, safeio/no-os-write checks, named codex symlink refusal test, phase seam call count, spec-to-epic call bound plus freshness invariant, AGENTS.md section grep/cross-reference, specInit RunE reuse, and go test ./.... I found no new falsifiability regression.
